skill.name: agentdb-advanced-features
skill.description: Guides advanced AgentDB usage for QUIC synchronization, multi-database layouts, distance metrics, hybrid search, and production operations.
skill.summary: Build distributed AgentDB systems with sync, sharding, hybrid search, and production patterns.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many command, network, database, and keyword patterns, but review shows the file is documentation with examples rather than executable skill code. Real residual risk comes from user-run operational commands, QUIC peer synchronization, local database manipulation, and a privileged firewall command. No evidence found of malicious intent, data exfiltration, credential theft, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: Privileged Firewall Command in Troubleshooting Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill includes a sudo ufw command to allow UDP traffic on port 4433. This is not malicious by itself, but copying it can change host firewall policy and expose a QUIC sync service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly present and requires elevated privileges. Context shows it is troubleshooting documentation, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Networked QUIC Peer Synchronization Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill shows enabling QUIC synchronization with peer addresses and environment variables. This can replicate AgentDB patterns across network peers if users configure real hosts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly enable network sync and list peer endpoints. The IPs are private examples, and no unauthorized destination or exfiltration logic is present.

medium_findings.title: User-Run CLI Commands Can Modify Local Databases
medium_findings.description: The documentation recommends npx agentdb and sqlite3 commands for querying, importing, merging, vacuuming, analyzing, and reindexing database files. These commands can execute external packages and modify local database state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are real shell commands with database side effects. They are legitimate AgentDB operations, but users should review package provenance and backups.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: Most external command findings are Markdown code block delimiters around TypeScript and bash examples. They do not execute when the skill is loaded.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the backticks delimit documentation examples. No executable Ruby code or automatic shell invocation was found.

low_findings.title: C2 and Weak Crypto Keyword Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The C2 and weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary text such as vec2, custom distance metrics, and QUIC encryption descriptions. No command-and-control behavior or weak cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive text or math examples. They do not implement cryptography or network control logic.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Private IP Addresses Are Example Topology Values
low_findings.description: The hardcoded IP addresses are private LAN examples for a three-node deployment. They are not external collection endpoints and do not indicate exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The addresses are RFC1918 private examples used consistently as Node 1, Node 2, and Node 3. No sensitive payload is sent to an untrusted remote service in the skill text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Privileged System Configuration Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The sudo firewall command can alter local network exposure and should be reviewed before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is present and privileged. It appears as user-facing troubleshooting guidance rather than automatic execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Synchronization Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: QUIC sync settings and peer lists can transmit database patterns between configured nodes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly enable sync and peer endpoints. Review found no hidden destination, credential use, or prompt injection.

content.value_statement: Distributed vector databases are difficult to tune, synchronize, and operate safely. This skill gives practical AgentDB patterns for sync, search, sharding, and production workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains QUIC synchronization settings for multi-node AgentDB deployments.
Compares cosine, Euclidean, dot product, and custom distance metrics.
Shows hybrid vector and metadata search patterns with filters and weights.
Describes multi-database separation, sharding, and query routing approaches.
Covers MMR, context synthesis, connection pooling, logging, and troubleshooting.
limitations: Does not include runnable helper scripts or automated validation tools.
Assumes users already understand Node.js, embeddings, and distributed systems.
Examples use placeholder hosts, vectors, and database paths that require adaptation.
Operational commands can modify databases or network settings if copied directly.

use_cases.title: Design distributed memory sync
use_cases.description: Plan AgentDB peer configuration for low-latency synchronization across application nodes.

use_cases.title: Improve semantic retrieval quality
use_cases.description: Choose distance metrics, hybrid filters, weights, and MMR settings for better search results.

use_cases.title: Operate production databases
use_cases.description: Review database export, merge, optimization, monitoring, and troubleshooting practices.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a distance metric
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AgentDB advanced features skill to compare cosine, Euclidean, and dot product metrics for my embedding workload. Recommend one metric and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Add hybrid search filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a hybrid AgentDB search query for documents with embeddings, year, category, and citation metadata. Include filter and weighting guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Plan multi-database routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to propose an AgentDB multi-database layout for knowledge, conversations, and code memories. Include sharding and routing considerations.

prompt_templates.title: Review QUIC sync readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit my AgentDB QUIC synchronization plan. Check peer configuration, firewall exposure, retry settings, monitoring, and rollback risks.

output_examples.input: I need faster AgentDB semantic search for recent research papers.
output_examples.output: A retrieval plan that combines vector similarity with year, category, and citation filters, plus guidance on k values and hybrid weights.

output_examples.input: I want to split memories across several AgentDB databases.
output_examples.output: A database layout with separate knowledge, conversation, and code stores, plus routing rules and operational notes.

output_examples.input: My QUIC sync deployment is not connecting across nodes.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering peer addresses, UDP port access, service logs, and safe review of firewall changes.

best_practices: Test AgentDB sync and database commands in a staging environment before production use.
Back up database files before import, merge, vacuum, analyze, or reindex operations.
Use explicit peer allowlists and review firewall changes before enabling QUIC synchronization.
anti_patterns: Do not copy sudo firewall commands without confirming the host security policy.
Do not use placeholder private IP addresses or hostnames in production configuration.
Do not run latest package commands against important databases without backups.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps design advanced AgentDB systems with distributed sync, hybrid search, sharding, and production operations.

faq.question: Does the skill execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guide with examples. Users choose whether to run any commands.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The guide includes network sync, database modification commands, and one privileged firewall command.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it cover hybrid search?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains vector search combined with metadata filters and weighted scoring.

faq.question: What should I verify before production use?
faq.answer: Verify peer hosts, firewall policy, package provenance, backups, and monitoring before deployment.
